elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, we classify smooth, contractible affine varieties equipped with faithful torus actions of complexity two, having a unique fixed point and a two-dimensional algebraic quotient isomorphic to a toric blow-up of a toric surface.
elements.sent-2411-002: These varieties are of particular interest as they represent the simplest candidates for potential counterexamples to the linearization conjecture in affine geometry.
elements.sent-2411-003: The linearization conjecture, a major open problem in affine geometry \cite{K96 first proposed by Kambayashi \cite{K79 , is stated as follows:.}}
elements.sent-2411-004: Let $G$ be a linearly reductive algebraic group acting regularly on $\mathbb{A ^{n}$.}
elements.sent-2411-005: Then, $G$ has a fixed point, say $P$, and the action of $G$ is linear with respect to a suitable coordinate system of $\mathbb{A ^{n}$ with $P$ as the origin.}
elements.sent-2411-006: In the two-dimensional affine space, Jung \cite{J42 and Gutwirth \cite{G62 proved the conjecture prior to Kambayashi's formal statement, and their work served as a precursor to the actual conjecture.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: For the affine spaces of dimention 3 and 4, Kraft, Popov, and Panyushev \cite{KP85,P83 established that every semisimple group action is linearizable.}
elements.sent-2411-008: Nevertheless, Schwarz \cite{S89 disproved this conjecture by providing examples of non-linearizable actions of the orthogonal group $\operatorname{O _{2}$ on $\mathbb{A ^{4}$ and of $\operatorname{SL _{2}$ on $\mathbb{A ^{7}$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-009: To date, counterexamples have been found for all connected reductive groups except tori \cite{K91 , as well as for many non-commutative finite groups \cite{MMJP91 .}}
elements.sent-2411-010: We restrict ourselves now to the case of tori.
elements.sent-2411-011: Let $X$ be an algebraic variety of dimension $n$ over the complex numbers $\mathbb{C}$.
elements.sent-2411-012: Let $T=\mathbb{G _m^{d}$ be the algebraic torus of dimension $d$.}
elements.sent-2411-013: The complexity of a $T$-action $\alpha$ on a variety $X$ is the codimension of the general orbit.
elements.sent-2411-014: If $\alpha$ is faithful, the complexity corresponds simply to $\dim(X) - \dim(T)=n-d$.
elements.sent-2411-015: In the case where the $T$-action has complexity 0, we say that $X$ is a toric variety.
elements.sent-2411-016: Białynicki-Birula showed that any codimension-one torus action in the affine space is linearizable \cite{BB67 .}
elements.sent-2411-017: And after a long series of papers by Kambayashi, Koras, Russell, Kaliman, and Makar-Limanov proved that the linearization conjecture holds for 1-dimensional torus acting on 3-dimensional affine space with complexity 2.. \cite{KR82,KR86,KR88.1,KR88.2,KR97,KML97,KKMLR97 .}
elements.sent-2411-018: Let us now review the state of the art for complexity-two torus actions on affine space.
elements.sent-2411-019: Independently, Koras-Russell \cite{KR88.1 and Kraft-Schwarz \cite{KS88,KS92 proved that if the fixed-point locus $F$ has dimension at least one, then the action is linearizable.}}
elements.sent-2411-020: The latter two authors, applying results by Bass-Haboush \cite{BH85 , showed that if the algebraic quotient $\mathbb{A ^n/\!/T$ is 1-dimensional, then it is isomorphic to the affine line $\mathbb{A ^1$, and moreover, that the set of fixed points is either a point or isomorphic to $\mathbb{A ^1$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: Finally, in the case where the action is unmixed, i.e., where the algebraic quotient is $0$-dimensional and provides a retraction to the fixed point set, the action is once again linearizable by \cite{KR82 .}
elements.sent-2411-022: Summarizing, in the case of complexity-two torus actions, the only remaining domain where counterexamples to the linearization conjecture may be found is for a torus action with an unique fixed point and an a $2$-dimensional algebraic quotient.
elements.sent-2411-023: Moreover, this algebraic quotient must be isomorphic to $\mathbb{A ^{2 /\!/\mu$, where $\mu$ is a finite cyclic group by \cite{GKR08 .}}}
elements.sent-2411-024: The aim of this paper is to prove the following theorem, which is a generalization of \cite[Theorem 4.1]{KR97 and \cite[Theorem 8]{P18 .}}
elements.sent-2411-025: A smooth, contractible affine variety $X$ with a faithful $T$-action of complexity two, a unique fixed point $x_0$, and an algebraic quotient isomorphic to $\mathbb{A ^{2 /\!/\mu$, where $\mu$ is a finite cyclic group, is determined by the following data:.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: The linear $T$-action on the tangent space $T_{x_0 X \simeq \mathbb{A ^{n}$ at the fixed point $x_0$.}}
elements.sent-2411-027: Two curves $C_1$ and $C_2$ in $\mathbb{A ^2$, invariant under $\mu$, each containing the origin and isomorphic to $\mathbb{A ^1$, intersect with only simple normal crossings.}}
elements.sent-2411-028: Recall that two curves $C_1, C_2$ in $\mathbb{A ^2$ are said to intersect only with simple normal crossing if, for every $x\in C_1\cap C_2$, there exists an analytic neighborhood $U$ of $x\subset \mathbb{A ^2$ such that $(U,C_1\cap U,C_2\cap U)$ is analytically isomorphic to $\mathbb{A ^2$ with the coordinate axes.}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: This section provides a brief overview of the combinatorial description of $T$-varieties, which will be used throughout the paper.
elements.sent-2411-030: A $T$-variety is a normal variety endowed with a faithful regular action of an algebraic torus $T$.
elements.sent-2411-031: The complexity of a $T$-variety is determined as the codimension of a general orbit under the $T$-action.
elements.sent-2411-032: Since the action is faithful, the complexity of a $T$-variety $X$ is given by $\dim X - \dim T$.
elements.sent-2411-033: The simplest examples of $T$-varieties are those of complexity zero, commonly referred to as toric varieties.
elements.sent-2411-034: These were initially introduced by Demazure in \cite{D70 .}
elements.sent-2411-035: Toric varieties are characterized by their combinatorial structure, which is encoded in certain collections of strongly convex rational polyhedral cones in $\mathbb{R ^n$, known as fans.For further details, refer to \cite{CLS11 .}}
elements.sent-2411-036: For $T$-varieties of higher complexity, a geometric-combinatorial description is also available.
elements.sent-2411-037: Altmann and Hausen developed such a description for affine $T$-varieties in \cite{AH06 , which we refer to as the AH-presentation.}
elements.sent-2411-038: Below, we provide a concise exposition of this presentation.
elements.sent-2411-039: An algebraic torus $T$ can be described via its character and $1$-parameter subgroup lattices, denoted $M$ and $N$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-040: The group $M$ is composed by characters, i.e., morphisms $u: T \to \mathbb{C ^*$, while $N$ consists of $1$-parameter subgroups, i.e., morphisms $p: \mathbb{C ^* \to T$.}}
elements.sent-2411-041: Both $M$ and $N$ are free abelian groups of rank $\dim T$, with a natural pairing $M \times N \to \mathbb{Z}$ given by $\langle u, p \rangle$, where $\langle u, p \rangle$ satisfies $u \circ p(t) = t^{\langle u, p \rangle}$ for all $t \in \mathbb{C ^*$.}
elements.sent-2411-042: We also let $M_\mathbb{R =M\otimes_\mathbb{Z \mathbb{R}$ and $N_\mathbb{R =N\otimes_\mathbb{Z \mathbb{R}$ be the corresponding real vector spaces and we extend the duality to them.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: The combinatorial nature of $T$-varieties derives from the well known fact that the comorphism $\mathbb{C [X]\rightarrow \mathbb{C [M]\otimes \mathbb{C [X]$ of the $T$-action on an affine $T$-variety induces an $M$-grading on $\mathbb{C [X]$ and, conversely, every $M$-grading on $\mathbb{C [X]$ arises in this way \cite[Section~2.1]{KR82 .}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-044: A toric variety is a $T$-variety of complexity zero, meaning it is a normal variety with a faithful $T$-action and an open dense orbit.
elements.sent-2411-045: Its combinatorial structure is captured by a fan $\Sigma$ in $N_\mathbb{R}$, which is a finite collection of strictly convex rational polyhedral cones closed under taking faces and intersections.
elements.sent-2411-046: For any cone $\sigma \in \Sigma$, the associated affine toric variety is $X(\sigma) = \operatorname{Spec \mathbb{C [\sigma^\vee \cap M]$, where $\sigma^\vee$ is the dual cone in $M_\mathbb{R}$ and $\mathbb{C [\sigma^\vee \cap M]$ is the semigroup algebra: \begin{equation}C[\sigma^\vee \cap M] = \bigoplus_{u \in \sigma^\vee \cap M} C \cdot \chi^u,\quad \chi^{u+u'} = \chi^u \cdot \chi^{u'}, \; \forall u, u' \in \sigma^\vee \cap M.\end{equation}.}}}
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}C[\sigma^\vee \cap M] = \bigoplus_{u \in \sigma^\vee \cap M} C \cdot \chi^u,\quad \chi^{u+u'} = \chi^u \cdot \chi^{u'}, \; \forall u, u' \in \sigma^\vee \cap M.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-047: Furthermore, if $\tau\subseteq\sigma$ is a face of $\sigma$, then the inclusion of algebras $\mathbb{C [\sigma^{\vee}\cap M]\hookrightarrow \mathbb{C [\tau^{\vee}\cap M]$ induces a $T$-equivariant open embedding $X(\tau)\hookrightarrow X(\sigma)$ of affine $T$-varieties.}}
elements.sent-2411-048: We define the toric variety $X(\Sigma)$ associated to the fan $\Sigma$ as the variety obtained by gluing the family $\{X(\sigma)\mid\sigma\in\Sigma\}$ along the open embeddings $X(\sigma)\hookleftarrow X(\sigma\cap\sigma')\hookrightarrow X(\sigma')$ for all $\sigma,\sigma'\in\Sigma$.
elements.sent-2411-049: Let $\sigma$ be a stronlgly convex rational polyhedral cone in $N_{\mathbb{R }$.}
elements.sent-2411-050: We let $\operatorname{Pol _{\sigma (N_{\mathbb{R )$ be the semigroup of all rational polyhedra with tail cone $\sigma$ under Minkowski sum.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-051: Let $Y$ be a normal semiprojective variety, i.e., $Y$ is projective over an affine variety.
elements.sent-2411-052: A polyhedral divisor on $Y$ is a formal sum $\mathcal{D =\sum_{Z}\Delta_Z\cdot Z$, where the sum runs trough all prime divisors $Z$ in $Y$, $\Delta_Z\in\operatorname{Pol _{\sigma (N_{\mathbb{R )$, and $\Delta_Z=\sigma$ for all but finitely many prime divisors $Z$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-053: We say that $\sigma$ is the tail cone of $\mathcal{D}$.
elements.sent-2411-054: For any $u\in\sigma^\vee\cap M$ we can evaluate $\mathcal{D}$ in $u$ by letting $\mathcal{D (u)$ be the $\mathbb{Q}$-divisor in $Y$ given by \begin{equation}D(u)=\sum_{Z\subseteq Y} \min\langle u,\Delta_Z\rangle \cdot Z\,.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-055: A polyhedral divisor $\mathcal{D}$ on $Y$ is called a p-divisor if $\mathcal{D (u)$ is semiample for all $u\in \sigma^\vee\cap M$ and big for all $u\in M$ contained in the relative interior of $\sigma^\vee$.}
elements.sent-2411-056: The AH-presentation of affine $T$-varieties is given in the following theorem \cite{AH06 .}
elements.sent-2411-057: To any p-divisor $\mathcal{D}$ on a normal semiprojective variety $Y$ one can associate an affine $T$-variety of complexity $\dim Y$ given by $X(Y,\mathcal{D )=\operatorname{Spec A(Y,\mathcal{D )$, where \begin{equation}A(Y,D)=\bigoplus_{u\in\sigma^{\vee}\cap M} A_u\chi^u,\quadand\quad A_u=H^0(Y,O(D(u))\,.\end{equation}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-058: Conversely, any affine $T$-variety is equivariantly isomorphic to $X(Y,\mathcal{D )$ for some p-divisor $\mathcal{D}$ on some normal semiprojective variety $Y$.}
elements.sent-2411-059: In this paper, we will only be interested in fully hyperbolic torus actions on $X=X(Y,\mathcal{D )$.}
elements.sent-2411-060: This corresponds to the case where $\sigma=\{0\}\in N_\mathbb{R}$ and there is a projective birational morphism $q\colon Y\to Y_0$ where $Y_0= \operatorname{Spec A_0$ is the spectrum of the ring of $T$-invariant regular functions on $X$.}
elements.sent-2411-061: Moreover, we denote by $\pi\colon X\to Y_0$ the natural map comming from the embedding $A_0\hookrightarrow A(Y,\mathcal{D )$.}
elements.sent-2411-062: Given a $T$-variety $X$, a method to determine a p-divisor $\mathcal{D}$ such that $X=X(Y,\mathcal{D )$ is given in \cite[section 11]{AH06 .}}
elements.sent-2411-063: The computation is reduced to the toric case by considering a $T$-equivariant embedding $X\hookrightarrow\mathbb{A ^{n}$, where $\mathbb{A ^{n}$ is consider with its unique faithful $\mathbb{G _m^n$-action up to conjugation.}}}
elements.sent-2411-064: More precisely, realizing $T$ as a subtorus $\mathbb{G _m^n$, we obtain an inclusion of lattices providing the following exact sequence. \begin{equation}\label{eq:AH} \xymatrix{0\ar[r] N\simeqZ^{k}\ar[r]_{F} N'\simeqZ^{n}\ar[r]_{P}\ar@/_{1pc}/[l]_{s} N'/N\ar[r] 0} ,\end{equation} where $F$ is the weight matrix is given by the induced action of $T$ on $\mathbb{A ^{n}$ and $s$ is a section of $F$.}}
elements.sent-2411-065: The quotient toric variety is determined by coarsest fan refining all the first integral vectors $v_{i}$ of the unidimensional cone generated by the $i$-th column vector of $P$ considered as rays in a $\mathbb{Z ^{n}$ lattice, and each $v_{i}$ correspond to a divisor.}

elements.sent-2411-067: The tail cone is $\sigma:=s(\mathbb{Q _{\geq0 ^{n}\cap F(\mathbb{Q ))$, and the polytopes are $\Delta_{i =s(\mathbb{R _{\geq0 ^{n}\cap P^{-1 (v_{i ))$, see \cite[section 11]{AH06 for further details.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-068: We first compute the AH-presentation of a fully hyperbolic linear $T$-action on the affine space.
elements.sent-2411-069: Let $X=\mathbb{A ^n$ be endowed with a linear fully hyperbolic $T$-action $\alpha$ of complexity 2 with 2-dimensional algebraic quotient with weight matrix $F$.}
elements.sent-2411-070: Then $X$ is equivariantly isomorphic to $X(Y,\mathcal{D )$ where $Y$ is a blow-up of $\mathbb{A ^{2 /\!/\mu$ and \begin{equation}D=\Delta_{1}\otimes H_{1}+\Delta_{2}\otimes H_{2}+\stackrel[i=3]{n}{\sum}\Delta_{i}\otimes E_{i}\,,\end{equation} where $H_1$ and $H_2$ are the strict transform of the images of the coordinate axes of $\mathbb{A ^2$ in $Y$, $E_i$ are the exceptional divisors of $Y$, and the polyhedral coefficients $\Delta_i$ are obtained from $F$ using \eqref{eq:AH .}}}}}
